title: Wright-Fisher Population Genetics
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Wright-Fisher Population Genetics: Wright-Fisher inference: estimate effective population size Ne, selection coefficient s, and mutation rates from allele frequency data. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.wf.wright_fisher_drift…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~100; population_structure_subdivision, bottleneck_demographic_history set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: p_{t+1} | p_t ~ Binomial(2Ne, p_t + s*p_t*(1-p_t)); allele frequencies drift under genetic drift and selection
E.world_state_x: effective population size Ne and selection coefficient s for each variant
E.observation_y: allele frequencies p(t) from time-series sequencing or cross-sectional population samples

W.regime: Existence of the recovered population_genetic_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 100); population_structure_subdivision dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Multinomial sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: log(Ne) uncertainty < 0.3 for 10+ generations of data. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~100. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |population_structure_subdivision|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
